verdict_reasoning: HSPA1A (HSP70-1/HSP72) is a cytoplasmic/nuclear chaperone under baseline conditions, but substantial published evidence documents its translocation to the extracellular leaflet of the plasma membrane under stress, oncogenic transformation, and certain activated immune states. Surface HSP70 on tumor cells has been detected by flow cytometry on intact, non-permeabilized cells and by surface biotinylation. It lacks a classical signal peptide or TM domain; its surface display involves an unconventional secretion/membrane-association mechanism (often with lipids, particularly ceramide-rich microdomains). Multiple HSP70-targeting therapeutic approaches (antibodies, NK cell-based therapies) exploit this tumor-surface pool. This is a documented cell-state-induced surface display: stress/transformation is required for stable PM presentation. Cell_state_induced is the best fit. Baseline: cytoplasmic chaperone. Contextual buckets: lysosomal_exocytosis and tissue_restricted_surface are less supported; the primary driver is stress/oncogenic transformation.
